   114‚Üí  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®h01, h12, h23‚ü© : (0 : Fin 4) < 1 ‚àß (1 : Fin 4) < 2 ‚àß (2 : Fin 4) < 3 := by simp
   115‚Üí    ¬∑ repeat' constructor
   116‚Üí      ¬∑ exact h.sign_oangle h01 h12
   117‚Üí      ¬∑ exact h.sign_oangle h12 h23
   118‚Üí      ¬∑ exact h.sign_oangle' (h01.trans h12) h23
   119‚Üí      ¬∑ exact h.sign_oangle'' h01 (h12.trans h23)
   120‚Üí  ¬∑ intro i j k hij hjk
   121‚Üí    fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> fin_cases k <;> simp at hij hjk
   122‚Üí    ¬∑ exact h1
   123‚Üí    ¬∑ rw [EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rotate_sign]
   124‚Üí      exact h4
   125‚Üí    ¬∑ rw [‚Üê EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rotate_sign]
   126‚Üí      exact h3
   127‚Üí    ¬∑ exact h2
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí@[simp]
   130‚Üítheorem isCcwConvexPolygon_four (A B C D : P) :
   131‚Üí    IsCcwConvexPolygon ![A, B, C, D] ‚Üî
   132‚Üí      (‚à° A B C).sign = 1 ‚àß (‚à° B C D).sign = 1 ‚àß (‚à° C D A).sign = 1 ‚àß (‚à° D A B).sign = 1 :=
   133‚Üí  isCcwConvexPolygon_four'
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí/-- The statement that a sequence of points form a convex polygon. -/
   136‚Üídef IsConvexPolygon {n : ‚Ñï} (p : Fin n ‚Üí P) : Prop :=
   137‚Üí  IsCcwConvexPolygon p ‚à® IsCcwConvexPolygon fun i => p (-i)
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí/-- Three affine independent points always form a convex polygon. -/
   140‚Üítheorem isConvexPolygon_three_of_affineIndependent {A B C : P}
   141‚Üí    (hABC : AffineIndependent ‚Ñù ![A, B, C]) : IsConvexPolygon ![A, B, C] := by
   142‚Üí  rw [‚Üê oangle_ne_zero_and_ne_pi_iff_affineIndependent, ‚Üê Real.Angle.sign_ne_zero_iff] at hABC
   143‚Üí  cases hsABC : (‚à° A B C).sign
   144‚Üí  ¬∑ exact (hABC hsABC).elim
   145‚Üí  ¬∑ right
   146‚Üí    intro i j k hij hjk
   147‚Üí    fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> fin_cases k <;> simp at hij hjk
   148‚Üí    rw [EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rev, Real.Angle.sign_neg, neg_eq_iff_eq_neg]
   149‚Üí    exact (oangle_rotate_sign A B C).trans hsABC
   150‚Üí  ¬∑ left
   151‚Üí    intro i j k hij hjk
   152‚Üí    fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> fin_cases k <;> simp at hij hjk
   153‚Üí    exact hsABC
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üítheorem isConvexPolygon_three_iff_affineIndependent {A B C : P} :
   156‚Üí    IsConvexPolygon ![A, B, C] ‚Üî AffineIndependent ‚Ñù ![A, B, C] := by
   157‚Üí  refine ‚ü®fun h => ?_, isConvexPolygon_three_of_affineIndependent‚ü©
   158‚Üí  rw [‚Üê oangle_ne_zero_and_ne_pi_iff_affineIndependent, ‚Üê Real.Angle.sign_ne_zero_iff]
   159‚Üí  let p := ![A, B, C]
   160‚Üí  change IsConvexPolygon p at h
   161‚Üí  change Real.Angle.sign (‚à° (p 0) (p 1) (p 2)) ‚âÝ 0
   162‚Üí  cases h with
   163‚Üí  | inl h =>
   164‚Üí    rw [h.sign_oangle (by simp) (by simp)]
   165‚Üí    rintro ‚ü®‚ü©
   166‚Üí  | inr h =>
   167‚Üí    suffices Real.Angle.sign (‚à° (p 0) (p 2) (p 1)) = 1 by rw [‚Üê oangle_swap‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ_sign, this]; rintro ‚ü®‚ü©
   168‚Üí    exact h.sign_oangle (i := 0) (j := 1) (k := 2) (by simp) (by simp)
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üítheorem isConvexPolygon_triangle (t : Affine.Triangle ‚Ñù P) : IsConvexPolygon t.points := by
   171‚Üí  have : t.points = ![t.points 0, t.points 1, t.points 2] := by ext i; fin_cases i <;> rfl
   172‚Üí  rw [this, isConvexPolygon_three_iff_affineIndependent, ‚Üê this]
   173‚Üí  exact t.independent
   174‚Üí
   175‚Üínoncomputable def triangle_area (a b c : P) : ‚Ñù :=
   176‚Üí  positiveOrientation.areaForm (a -·µ• c) (b -·µ• c) / 2
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üílemma triangle_area_eq_det (a b c : ‚Ñù¬≤) :
   179‚Üí    triangle_area a b c =
   180‚Üí    Matrix.det !![a 0, b 0, c 0;
   181‚Üí                  a 1, b 1, c 1;
   182‚Üí                  1,   1,   1] / 2 := by
   183‚Üí  rw [triangle_area, Orientation.areaForm_to_volumeForm,
   184‚Üí    positiveOrientation.volumeForm_robust (EuclideanSpace.basisFun (Fin 2) ‚Ñù) rfl, Module.Basis.det_apply]
   185‚Üí  suffices (a 0 - c 0) * (b 1 - c 1) - (b 0 - c 0) * (a 1 - c 1) =
   186‚Üí      a 0 * b 1 - a 0 * c 1 - b 0 * a 1 + b 0 * c 1 + c 0 * a 1 - c 0 * b 1 by
   187‚Üí    simp [Matrix.det_fin_two, Matrix.det_fin_three, Module.Basis.toMatrix, this]
   188‚Üí  ring
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí/--
   191‚ÜíGiven a finite set of points in the plane, we define the number of distinct distances between pairs
   192‚Üíof points.
   193‚Üí-/
   194‚Üínoncomputable def distinctDistances (points : Finset ‚Ñù¬≤) : ‚Ñï :=
   195‚Üí  (points.offDiag.image fun (pair : ‚Ñù¬≤ √ó ‚Ñù¬≤) => dist pair.1 pair.2).card
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üíend EuclideanGeometry
   198‚Üí

   200‚Üídef IsIsosceles {Œ± : Type*} [Dist Œ±] (p q r : Œ±) : Prop :=
   201‚Üí  dist p q = dist q r ‚à® dist q r = dist r p ‚à® dist r p = dist p q
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üínonrec def Set.IsIsosceles {Œ± : Type} [Dist Œ±] (A : Set Œ±) :=
   204‚Üí  Nonempty A ‚àß A.Triplewise (IsIsosceles ¬∑ ¬∑ ¬∑)

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 991
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/991](https://www.erdosproblems.com/991)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíSuppose $A = \{w_1, \ldots, w_n\} \subset S^2$ maximises $\prod_{i<j} |w_i - w_j|$ over all
    25‚Üípossible sets of size $n$ (such sets are called Fekete points).
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíIs it true that
    28‚Üí$$
    29‚Üí  \max_C \left| |A \cap C| - \alpha_C \cdot n \right| = o(n),
    30‚Üí$$
    31‚Üíwhere the maximum is taken over all spherical caps $C$ and $\alpha_C$ is the area
    32‚Üíof $C$ (normalised so that the entire sphere has area $1$)?
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThis was proved. Brauchart [Br08] showed $O(n^{3/4})$ and Marzo‚ÄìMas [MaMa21]
    35‚Üíimproved this to $O(n^{2/3})$.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[Br08] Brauchart, J. S., *Optimal discrete Riesz energy and discrepancy*,
    38‚ÜíUniform Distribution Theory 3 (2008), no. 1, 47‚Äì78.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[MaMa21] Marzo, J. and Mas, A., *Discrepancy of minimal Riesz energy points*,
    41‚ÜíJ. Approx. Theory 264 (2021), 105538.
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üíopen Classical Finset BigOperators
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üínamespace Erdos991
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- The product of pairwise distances of a finite set of points. -/
    49‚Üínoncomputable def pairwiseDistProd (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 3))) : ‚Ñù :=
    50‚Üí  ‚àè p ‚àà P.offDiag, ‚Äñp.1 - p.2‚Äñ
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- A finite set of points on $S^2$ maximises the pairwise distance product
    53‚Üíamong all sets of the same cardinality on $S^2$. -/
    54‚Üídef IsMaxPairwiseDist (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 3))) : Prop :=
    55‚Üí  (‚àÄ p ‚àà P, ‚Äñp‚Äñ = 1) ‚àß
    56‚Üí  ‚àÄ (Q : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 3))),
    57‚Üí    (‚àÄ q ‚àà Q, ‚Äñq‚Äñ = 1) ‚Üí Q.card = P.card ‚Üí
    58‚Üí    pairwiseDistProd Q ‚â§ pairwiseDistProd P
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/--
    61‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 991 [Er64b]:
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíIf $A = \{w_1, \ldots, w_n\} \subset S^2$ are Fekete points (maximising the product of
    64‚Üípairwise distances), then the spherical cap discrepancy of $A$ is $o(n)$:
    65‚Üífor every $\varepsilon > 0$, if $n$ is large enough and $P$ is any set of $n$ Fekete points,
    66‚Üíthen for every spherical cap $C(v,t) = \{x \in S^2 : \langle x,v \rangle \geq t\}$,
    67‚Üí$$
    68‚Üí  \left| |P \cap C(v,t)| - \frac{1-t}{2} \cdot n \right| \leq \varepsilon \cdot n.
    69‚Üí$$
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíHere $\frac{1-t}{2}$ is the normalised surface area of the cap $C(v,t)$ on $S^2$.
    72‚Üí-/
    73‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 31 52]
    74‚Üítheorem erdos_991 :
    75‚Üí    answer(True) ‚Üî
    76‚Üí    ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
    77‚Üí    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 3))),
    78‚Üí      IsMaxPairwiseDist P ‚Üí
    79‚Üí      P.card ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    80‚Üí      ‚àÄ (v : EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 3)) (t : ‚Ñù),
    81‚Üí        ‚Äñv‚Äñ = 1 ‚Üí -1 ‚â§ t ‚Üí t ‚â§ 1 ‚Üí
    82‚Üí        |((P.filter (fun x => @inner ‚Ñù _ _ x v ‚â• t)).card : ‚Ñù) -
    83‚Üí          (1 - t) / 2 * (P.card : ‚Ñù)| ‚â§ Œµ * (P.card : ‚Ñù) := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíend Erdos991

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 988
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/988](https://www.erdosproblems.com/988)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $P \subseteq S^2$ is a subset of the unit sphere then define the discrepancy
    25‚Üí$$D(P) = \max_C \left| |C \cap P| - \alpha_C |P| \right|,$$
    26‚Üíwhere the maximum is taken over all spherical caps $C$, and $\alpha_C$ is the
    27‚Üíappropriately normalised measure of $C$.
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíIs it true that $\min_{|P|=n} D(P) \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$?
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíThis was proved (in any number of dimensions) by Schmidt [Sc69b].
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Er64b] Erd≈ës, P., *On some problems in number theory*, 1964.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Sc69b] Schmidt, W. M., *Irregularities of distribution. IV.* Invent. Math. 7
    36‚Üí(1969), 55‚Äì82.
    37‚Üí-/

    41‚Üínamespace Erdos988
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 988 [Er64b]:
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíFor every $M > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for every finite set $P$ of points
    47‚Üíon the unit sphere $S^2$ in $\mathbb{R}^3$ with $|P| \geq N_0$, there exists a spherical cap
    48‚Üí$C(v,t) = \{x \in S^2 : \langle x, v \rangle \geq t\}$ (with $v$ a unit vector and
    49‚Üí$t \in [-1,1]$) such that the discrepancy
    50‚Üí$\left||C(v,t) \cap P| - \frac{1-t}{2} \cdot |P|\right| \geq M$.
    51‚Üí
    52‚ÜíHere $\frac{1-t}{2}$ is the normalised surface area measure of the cap $C(v,t)$ on $S^2$.
    53‚Üí-/
    54‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11 52]
    55‚Üítheorem erdos_988 :
    56‚Üí    answer(True) ‚Üî
    57‚Üí    ‚àÄ M : ‚Ñù, M > 0 ‚Üí
    58‚Üí    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 3))),
    59‚Üí      (‚àÄ p ‚àà P, ‚Äñp‚Äñ = 1) ‚Üí
    60‚Üí      P.card ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    61‚Üí      ‚àÉ (v : EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 3)) (t : ‚Ñù),
    62‚Üí        ‚Äñv‚Äñ = 1 ‚àß -1 ‚â§ t ‚àß t ‚â§ 1 ‚àß
    63‚Üí        |((P.filter (fun x => @inner ‚Ñù _ _ x v ‚â• t)).card : ‚Ñù) -
    64‚Üí          (1 - t) / 2 * (P.card : ‚Ñù)| ‚â• M := by
    65‚Üí  sorry
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üíend Erdos988

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 227
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíLet $f = \sum a_n z^n$ be an entire function which is not a polynomial. Is it true that if
    23‚Üíthe limit of the ratio of the maximum term to the maximum modulus exists, then it must be $0$?
    24‚ÜíThis was disproved by Clunie and Hayman [ClHa64], who showed the limit can take any value
    25‚Üíin $[0, 1/2]$.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/227](https://www.erdosproblems.com/227)
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[ClHa64] Clunie, J. and Hayman, W. K., *The spherical derivative of integral and
    30‚Üímeromorphic functions*, Comment. Math. Helv. 40 (1966), 117-148.
    31‚Üí-/
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üíopen Complex Filter
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen scoped Topology
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos227
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/--
    40‚ÜíThe maximum term of a power series with coefficients $a$ at radius $r$:
    41‚Üí$\mu(r) = \sup_n \|a_n\| \cdot r^n$
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üínoncomputable def maxTerm (a : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ) (r : ‚Ñù) : ‚Ñù :=
    44‚Üí  ‚®Ü n : ‚Ñï, ‚Äña n‚Äñ * r ^ n
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚ÜíThe maximum modulus of $f$ on the circle of radius $r$:
    48‚Üí$M(r) = \sup\{\|f(z)\| \mid \|z\| = r\}$
    49‚Üí-/
    50‚Üínoncomputable def maxModulus (f : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ) (r : ‚Ñù) : ‚Ñù :=
    51‚Üí  sSup {x : ‚Ñù | ‚àÉ z : ‚ÑÇ, ‚Äñz‚Äñ = r ‚àß x = ‚Äñf z‚Äñ}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 227 (Disproved):
    55‚ÜíLet $f = \sum a_n z^n$ be an entire function which is not a polynomial. Is it true that if
    56‚Üí$$\lim_{r \to \infty} \frac{\max_n |a_n| r^n}{\max_{|z|=r} |f(z)|}$$
    57‚Üíexists then it must be $0$?
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíThis was disproved by Clunie and Hayman [ClHa64], who showed that the limit can take
    60‚Üíany value in $[0, 1/2]$.
    61‚Üí-/
    62‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 30]
    63‚Üítheorem erdos_227 : answer(False) ‚Üî
    64‚Üí    ‚àÄ (f : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ) (a : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ),
    65‚Üí      (‚àÄ z : ‚ÑÇ, HasSum (fun n => a n * z ^ n) (f z)) ‚Üí

    24‚ÜíA finite set $A \subset \mathbb{R}^n$ is called Ramsey if, for any $k \geq 1$, there exists some
    25‚Üí$d = d(A,k)$ such that in any $k$-colouring of $\mathbb{R}^d$ there exists a monochromatic

    29‚Üíthat every Ramsey set is 'spherical': it lies on the surface of some sphere.
    30‚ÜíGraham has conjectured that every spherical set is Ramsey, which would give a
    31‚Üícomplete characterisation.

    34‚Üínon-degenerate simplices [FrRo90], trapezoids [Kr92], and regular
    35‚Üípolygons/polyhedra [Kr91].

    52‚Üíisometric copy of $A$ (i.e., a copy obtained by a distance-preserving map). -/

    34‚Üí  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ‚Ñù)) spanningTrees)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := Fintype.card Œ±
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚Ñù)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset Œ±) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set Œ±))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset Œ±) : ‚Ñ§ := (s.card : ‚Ñ§) - (‚ãÉ v ‚àà (s : Set Œ±), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚Ñù)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚àß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ñï :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : Œ± => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚àß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚Ñù)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum

    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=

    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    91‚Üí  (‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚Ñù¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚Ñù¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast

   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚àÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚àß (‚àÄ p ‚àà P, p.IsPath) ‚àß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum

   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v

   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
